title: Stop storing a User struct in Oban args
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/struct_args_worker.ex` passes a `%User{}` struct and a `%DateTime{}` directly into Oban args. Structs serialize to plain maps during JSON encoding, losing their `__struct__` field — the perform/1 pattern match on `%User{}` will crash on retry. Rewrite the module so the enqueue helper passes `%{"user_id" => user.id, "deliver_at" => DateTime.to_iso8601(deliver_at), "channel" => "push"}` and perform/1 loads the user via `Accounts.get_user!/1` and parses the deliver_at with `DateTime.from_iso8601/1`. Write the result to `lib/my_app/workers/notification_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Single-step transformation once the problem is named; the fix (load by id, ISO8601 the datetime) is one of the canonical Oban idioms.
research_citation: oliver-kriska/claude-elixir-phoenix Iron Laws — 'Storing Elixir structs directly in job args'
